backlog.title: weave v1.0 — Manifest-free extension path printer
backlog.description: One-shot build of the `weave` Go CLI: resolves a human-friendly extension tag to the absolute filesystem path of a locally-stored pi.dev extension, for consumption by `pi -e <path>`. Manifest-free (disk-discovered), never in a pi auto-discovery location, loaded only via explicit `-e`. Covers the full PRD: Go CLI (§6), discovery+classification+resolution (§7), extensions-dir location (§8), validation (§9), one example extension (§11), install.sh (§12), README (§15), and shell completions (§14). Build order follows PRD §18: extensions-dir location first (hardest), then discovery, resolution, search/check, CLI polish, then packaging/docs. Research findings live in plan/001_19b4b465824d/architecture/.

milestones.title: Foundation, config & extdir location (PRD §8, build order step 1)
milestones.description: Establish the buildable Go skeleton (go.mod, .gitignore, LICENSE) and implement the single most failure-prone area: the settings config file (§8.1) and locating the extensions/ dir via the §8.3 priority order (env var → config → sibling-of-binary via os.Executable+EvalSymlinks → walk-up-from-cwd). Prove it with a minimal `weave --path` / `weave --version`. Also build the full parseArgs() arg parser so later milestones only add dispatch branches, not parser changes.

tasks.title: Repo & Go module scaffolding
tasks.description: Create the buildable project skeleton: go.mod (module github.com/dabstractor/weave, zero dependencies), .gitignore (PRD §16), and LICENSE (MIT). This unblocks every subsequent Go package.

subtasks.title: go.mod, .gitignore, LICENSE
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_19b4b465824d/architecture/codebase_state.md — repo is greenfield (only PRD.md + .git + plan/). Go 1.26.4 toolchain present. Module path: github.com/dabstractor/weave. ZERO third-party deps (no go.sum).
2. INPUT: None (first subtask). PRD.md is read-only reference.
3. LOGIC: Create `go.mod` with `module github.com/dabstractor/weave` and `go 1.25` directive (latest-two-stable policy; toolchain is 1.26.4 so 1.25 is safe). NO `require` block — zero deps. Create `.gitignore` with EXACTLY PRD §16 content: `/weave`, `/dist`, `*.test`, `*.out`, `.DS_Store`. Create `LICENSE` as standard MIT license text (PRD §5/§19 — MIT, match skilldozer/mcpeepants conventions). Run `go build ./...` to confirm the module compiles (empty but valid). DO NOT create main.go or any internal/ package here.
4. OUTPUT: A compilable Go module skeleton (go.mod, .gitignore, LICENSE). `go build ./...` succeeds (empty module).
5. DOCS: none — no user-facing/config/API surface change (LICENSE/.gitignore are repo hygiene, not documented).

tasks.title: internal/config — settings file (PRD §8.1, hand-rolled YAML)
tasks.description: Implement the settings sidecar that records ONLY the store location. Unlike skilldozer (which used yaml.v3), weave hand-rolls a trivial parser for the one key `store:` — zero dependencies. Includes Path() (config file location) and DefaultStore() (XDG data home default).

subtasks.title: File struct + hand-rolled Load/Save + Path() + DefaultStore()
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_19b4b465824d/architecture/architecture_mapping.md §1. skilldozer's config used yaml.v3; weave hand-rolls a ~10-line line scanner for the one key `store:`. Env var is lowercase: `weave_CONFIG` (PRD §8.1: `weave_CONFIG=<file>`).
2. INPUT: go.mod from P1.M1.T1.S1.
3. LOGIC: Create `internal/config/config.go`. Define `type File struct { Store string }`. Implement:
  - `Load(path string) (File, error)`: Read file via `os.ReadFile`. Scan lines for `^store:\s*(.+)$` (trim trailing whitespace). Return `File{Store: value}` if found, else `File{}`. Return the raw `os.ReadFile` error VERBATIM (so callers can `errors.Is(err, fs.ErrNotExist)` — same convention as skilldozer). A present-but-no-store-line file returns `File{}, nil` (not an error — treated as 'not configured').
  - `Save(path string, f File) error`: MkdirAll parent (0o755), WriteFile with exactly `store: <value>\n` (0o644). Deterministic output.
  - `Path() (string, error)`: If `os.Getenv("weave_CONFIG")` is non-empty, return `filepath.Clean(value)`. Else `filepath.Join(os.UserConfigDir(), "weave", "config.yaml")`. Return `os.UserConfigDir()` error verbatim.
  - `DefaultStore() (string, error)`: If `XDG_DATA_HOME` is set and absolute → `$XDG_DATA_HOME/weave/extensions`. Else `filepath.Join(os.UserHomeDir(), ".local", "share", "weave", "extensions")`.
  Mock/replace nothing — pure stdlib functions of env vars.
4. OUTPUT: Package `config` with File/Load/Save/Path/DefaultStore, consumed by extdir.findConfig (P1.M1.T3) and main.runInit (P1.M4.T4). All are pure functions of env/filesystem.
5. DOCS: none — internal package; `weave_CONFIG` env var is documented in README (Mode B final task P1.M6.T5).

tasks.title: internal/extdir — locate the extensions dir (PRD §8.3 priority order)
tasks.description: Implement the hardest, most failure-prone component: resolving the extensions/ directory via the 5-rule priority order (first hit wins). Must be symlink-aware so symlink installs resolve back to the repo. Port skilldozer's internal/skillsdir nearly verbatim — only the env-var name, sibling dir name (`extensions`), and the store predicate change.

subtasks.title: Source type + env-var resolution (rule 1) + HasExtensionEntry predicate
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_19b4b465824d/architecture/architecture_mapping.md §2. Port skilldozer's internal/skillsdir/skillsdir.go. The env var is `weave_EXTENSIONS_DIR` (lowercase). The sibling dir name is `extensions` (not `skills`). The store predicate is `HasExtensionEntry` (not `HasSkillMD`).
2. INPUT: config package from P1.M1.T2.S1 (for findConfig in S2).
3. LOGIC: Create `internal/extdir/extdir.go`. Define `type Source int` with constants `SourceEnv`, `SourceConfig`, `SourceSibling`, `SourceWalkUp`. Implement `String()` returning labels: `"weave_EXTENSIONS_DIR"`, `"config file"`, `"sibling of binary"`, `"ancestor of cwd"`. Define `const envVar = "weave_EXTENSIONS_DIR"`.
  Implement rule 1 (`findEnv`): Read `os.LookupEnv(envVar)`; if set, `os.Stat` it; if existing dir, return `(absPath, SourceEnv, true)` via `filepath.Abs`. If unset/empty/not-a-dir, return `("", 0, false)`. Do NOT EvalSymlinks the env path.
  Implement `HasExtensionEntry(dir string) bool`: Walk `dir` with `filepath.WalkDir`; return true as soon as ANY extension entry is found at any depth. An extension entry is: (a) a `*.ts` or `*.js` FILE whose base name is NOT `index.ts`/`index.js`, OR (b) a DIRECTORY containing `index.ts`/`index.js`, OR (c) a DIRECTORY with `package.json` containing a `pi.extensions` array. Use a sentinel error to short-circuit on first find. This is the `init` cwd-auto-detect predicate AND the walk-up qualifier.
4. OUTPUT: Package `extdir` with Source type, envVar constant, findEnv helper, and HasExtensionEntry (exported). Feeds Find() in P1.M1.T3.S3.
5. DOCS: none — internal package; `weave_EXTENSIONS_DIR` env var documented in README (Mode B final task).

subtasks.title: Config-based resolution (rule 2) + sibling-of-binary (rule 3, symlink-aware)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_19b4b465824d/architecture/architecture_mapping.md §2 and pi_extension_facts.md. Port skilldozer's findConfig and findSibling/resolveSiblingFromExe verbatim with renames. EvalSymlinks is REQUIRED on macOS (redundant on Linux via /proc/self/exe) — do NOT drop it.
2. INPUT: Source type, envVar, HasExtensionEntry from P1.M1.T3.S1. config package from P1.M1.T2.S1.
3. LOGIC: Implement rule 2 (`findConfig`): Call `config.Path()` for the bootstrap path. If error, return miss. Call `config.Load(path)`. If error or `f.Store == ""`, return miss. Resolve relative store against `filepath.Dir(path)` (config file's dir). If the resolved store is an existing dir, return `(store, SourceConfig, true)`. Never errors — any failure falls through.
  Implement rule 3 (`findSibling` + `resolveSiblingFromExe`): `exe, err := os.Executable()`; if err, skip. `real, err := filepath.EvalSymlinks(exe)`; if err, `real = exe`. `repoDir := filepath.Dir(real)`. `candidate := filepath.Join(repoDir, "extensions")`. If `os.Stat(candidate)` is an existing dir, return `(candidate, SourceSibling, true)`. This is what makes `~/.local/bin/weave -> ~/projects/weave/weave` resolve back to the repo.
  TEST: create a temp binary with a sibling `extensions/` dir AND a symlink to that binary in a DIFFERENT temp dir; running via the symlink must resolve to the real binary's repo dir. Use `t.TempDir()` + `os.Symlink`.
4. OUTPUT: Internal helpers for rules 2 and 3, feeding Find() in S3.
5. DOCS: none — internal package; symlink-install behavior documented in README (Mode B final task).

subtasks.title: Walk-up-from-cwd (rule 4) + Find() combiner + ErrNotFound
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Rule 4 exists for `go run` / dev where binary is in a temp dir and rules 1-3 fail. Port skilldozer's findWalkUp/findWalkUpAncestor verbatim with predicate change.
2. INPUT: Rules 1-3 helpers from P1.M1.T3.S1/S2. HasExtensionEntry from S1.
3. LOGIC: Implement rule 4 (`findWalkUp` + `findWalkUpAncestor`): Start at `os.Getwd()`. Ascend via `filepath.Dir` until filesystem root. For each ancestor (including start), if `<ancestor>/extensions` exists AND `HasExtensionEntry(candidate)` returns true, return `(candidate, SourceWalkUp, true)`. A dir with `extensions/` but no entries keeps ascending.
  Implement `var ErrNotFound = errors.New("weave is not configured; run \`weave init\`")`. This is the exact PRD §8.2/§6.4 user-facing one-line fix.
  Implement `func Find() (dir string, src Source, err error)`: Try rule 1 → rule 2 → rule 3 → rule 4 in order; return first hit as `(absDir, src, nil)`. If all miss, return `("", 0, ErrNotFound)`. NEVER prompts (PRD §8.2 prompt safety).
  TEST: temp dir tree with extensions/ at various depths; chdir into a subdir and confirm walk-up finds it. Confirm Find() returns ErrNotFound when all rules miss (neutralize env + config in tests — see skilldozer's unsetEnvVar helper pattern).
4. OUTPUT: `extdir.Find()` — the public entrypoint consumed by main.go (P1.M1.T4) and discover.Index (P1.M2.T3). Returns (absDir, Source, err).
5. DOCS: none — internal package; resolution rules documented in README §7 section (Mode B final task).

tasks.title: main.go entrypoint — parseArgs skeleton + run() dispatch (--path, --version)
tasks.description: Build main.go with the FULL parseArgs() arg parser (all §6.1/§6.2 flags, short bundles, =-forms, init/check subcommands, unknown flag capture) so later milestones only add dispatch branches to run(). Initially dispatch only --version and --path to prove location resolution works.

subtasks.title: config struct + parseArgs (full flag matrix) + run() dispatch (--version, --path)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_19b4b465824d/architecture/architecture_mapping.md §8. Port skilldozer's main.go structure. The `version` var is overridden at build time via ldflags: `-X main.version=$(git describe --tags --always 2>/dev/null || echo dev)`. Default is "dev".
2. INPUT: extdir.Find() from P1.M1.T3.S3.
3. LOGIC: Create `main.go` with `package main`. Implement:
  - `var version = "dev"` (package-level string var, NOT const — ldflags requirement).
  - `type config struct` with ALL §6.1/§6.2 fields: version, help, path, list, all, file, relative, noColor, searchMode, searchQ, check, init, initStore, tags []string, unknownFlag string.
  - `func parseArgs(args []string) config`: Hand-rolled index-based loop (NOT Go's flag package). Handle: long flags (--version, --help, --path, --list, --all, --file, --relative, --no-color, --search, --store); short forms (-v, -h, -p, -l, -a, -f, -s); `=`-forms (--flag=value); short bundles (-vpl, -sfoo); value-taking flags (--search <q>, --store <dir> consume next token via i++); reserved subcommand tokens (check, init); positional tags (non-dashed tokens → c.tags); unknown dashed flags → capture first into c.unknownFlag (M5 turns this into exit 2). Port `expandShortBundle` from skilldozer verbatim (two-phase validate-then-commit).
  - `func run(args []string, stdout, stderr io.Writer) int`: Dispatch only --version (print `weave <version>\n` to stdout, exit 0) and --path (call extdir.Find; on success print dir to stdout + `(found via <src>)` to stderr; on failure print err to stderr, exit 1). All other modes are no-ops for now (added in later milestones). Return exit code.
  - `var isTerminal = func(w io.Writer) bool` (type-assert to *os.File, check ModeCharDevice).
  - `func main() { os.Exit(run(os.Args[1:], os.Stdout, os.Stderr)) }`.
  TEST: run([]string{"--version"}) returns 0 and stdout contains "weave". run([]string{"--path"}) returns 0 and stdout contains the extensions dir path (with sibling-of-binary rule). run([]string{"--path"}) in unconfigured env returns 1.
4. OUTPUT: Compilable `weave` binary supporting `weave --version` and `weave --path`. parseArgs handles the full flag matrix (later milestones add dispatch branches to run, not parser changes).
5. DOCS: none — CLI surface (`--version`, `--path`) is documented in README (Mode B final task). The `version` var JSDoc is inline in main.go.

milestones.title: Extension discovery, classification & metadata (PRD §7.1, §7.3, build order step 2)
milestones.description: Implement the core new logic vs skilldozer: the classify-then-descend discovery walk that recognizes single-file, dir, and package extensions; the JSDoc comment extractor; package.json metadata parsing; and the --list table UI. This is where 'an extension is a file OR a dir' diverges from skilldozer's 'a skill is always a directory with SKILL.md.'

tasks.title: internal/discover — Extension struct + metadata extraction (PRD §7.3)
tasks.description: Define the Extension type (replaces skilldozer's Skill) and the metadata extraction from package.json (JSON) and leading JSDoc comment. This is the data model that discovery, resolve, search, check, and ui all consume.

subtasks.title: Extension struct + package.json parsing + BuildExtension
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_19b4b465824d/architecture/architecture_mapping.md §3a. Extension replaces skilldozer's Skill. Metadata from package.json via encoding/json (NOT yaml). PRD §7.3 defines the fields and priority order. PRD §10 shows the package.json schema.
2. INPUT: go.mod from P1.M1.T1.S1.
3. LOGIC: Create `internal/discover/extension.go`. Define:
  ```go
  type Extension struct {
      Path           string  // resolvable path (file for files, dir for dirs) — DEFAULT output
      EntryFile      string  // the .ts/.js file pi loads — --file output
      RelTag         string  // canonical tag: path relative to extensions/, .ts/.js stripped for single files
      Kind           string  // "file" | "dir" | "package"
      Name           string  // package.json name, else ""
      Description    string  // package.json description, else leading JSDoc, else ""
      Keywords       []string // package.json keywords
      Category       string  // package.json weave.category
      Aliases        []string // package.json weave.aliases
      HasPackageJSON bool    // whether a package.json was read
  }
  ```
  Define a `packageJSON` struct for JSON unmarshaling: `Name`, `Description` (string), `Keywords` ([]string), `Pi` (struct with `Extensions` []string), `Weave` (struct with `Aliases` []string, `Category` string), `Dependencies` (map[string]string).
  Implement `parsePackageJSON(dir string) (packageJSON, bool, error)`: Read `dir/package.json` via os.ReadFile + json.Unmarshal. Return `(pkg, true, nil)` on success. If file doesn't exist, return `(packageJSON{}, false, nil)`. If JSON parse fails, return `(packageJSON{}, true, parseError)`. Be lenient: unknown keys ignored; missing optional fields are zero-valued.
  Implement `BuildExtension(path, entryFile, relTag, kind string, pkg packageJSON, hasPkg bool, jsdocDesc string) Extension`: Assemble from parsed data. Description = pkg.Description if non-empty, else jsdocDesc, else "". Name = pkg.Name. Keywords = pkg.Keywords. Category = pkg.Weave.Category. Aliases = pkg.Weave.Aliases.
  Port skilldozer's `toStringSlice` helper if needed for []any normalization (encoding/json unmarshals []string fields directly into []string when the struct field is typed, so this may be unnecessary — but keep it for defensive []any→[]string coercion from nested weave.aliases if they come as []any).
4. OUTPUT: `discover.Extension` type + `parsePackageJSON` + `BuildExtension`. Consumed by Index() (P1.M2.T3), resolve (P1.M3.T1), search (P1.M4.T1), check (P1.M4.T2), ui (P1.M2.T4).
5. DOCS: none — internal types; the Extension metadata model is documented in README §5/§6 (Mode B final task).

subtasks.title: JSDoc comment extractor (ExtractJSDoc)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_19b4b465824d/architecture/architecture_mapping.md §3b. This replaces skilldozer's ParseFrontmatter (YAML). Extensions have no frontmatter format — the leading JSDoc `/** ... */` is the standard metadata home for single-file extensions. PRD §7.3 item 2 defines the extraction rule precisely.
2. INPUT: Extension struct from P1.M2.T1.S1.
3. LOGIC: Implement `func ExtractJSDoc(path string) string` in `internal/discover/extension.go` (or a separate `jsdoc.go`):
  1. Read file via `os.ReadFile(path)`. On error, return "".
  2. Strip leading UTF-8 BOM: `data = bytes.TrimPrefix(data, []byte{0xEF, 0xBB, 0xBF})`.
  3. Convert to string, split into lines.
  4. Skip leading whitespace and blank lines.
  5. If the first non-blank line does NOT start with exactly `/**` → return "" (not a JSDoc block; could be `//`, `/*`, or code).
  6. Find the closing `*/`. Scan lines (or scan the string) for `*/`. If no closing `*/` → return "".
  7. Take the content between `/**` and `*/`. For multi-line: for each line, strip an optional leading run of whitespace followed by a single `*` (and one optional space after it). Drop empty lines. Join survivors with a single space. Trim.
  8. Single-line `/** ... */`: take content between `/**` and `*/`, trim. Works with no inner `*` lines.
  TEST: multi-line JSDoc with ` * ` prefixes; single-line `/** desc */`; no JSDoc (just code); `//` line comment; `/* plain block */`; BOM-prefixed file; file with leading blank lines before JSDoc; unclosed `/**` (no `*/`).
4. OUTPUT: `ExtractJSDoc(path) string` — used by Index() to populate Description when package.json has no description. Also used by check to determine 'no description' WARN.
5. DOCS: none — internal function; JSDoc convention documented in README §5/§6 (Mode B final task).

tasks.title: internal/discover — classify-then-descend walk logic (PRD §7.1)
tasks.description: Implement the core new logic: classifying an on-disk entry as a single-file, dir, or package extension, and the recursion rule that prevents double-counting (don't descend into recognized extension dirs). This is the extensions analog of skilldozer's 'a SKILL.md makes its dir a skill, full stop.'

subtasks.title: classifyEntry + classify-then-descend recursion rules
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_19b4b465824d/architecture/architecture_mapping.md §3c and pi_extension_facts.md §4-5. The classify-then-descend rule (PRD §7.1 item 3) is LOAD-BEARING — it prevents double-counting a dir extension's internal helper files as separate entries. pi's own loader treats a dir as one unit; weave must match.
2. INPUT: Extension struct, parsePackageJSON, BuildExtension, ExtractJSDoc from P1.M2.T1.S1/S2.
3. LOGIC: Create `internal/discover/discover.go`. Implement the classification logic:
  Define a helper `isExtensionFile(name string) bool`: `strings.HasSuffix(name, ".ts") || strings.HasSuffix(name, ".js")`.
  Define `classifyFile(root, path string) (*Extension, bool)`: Given a FILE entry: if `isExtensionFile(name)` AND name is NOT `index.ts`/`index.js` → it's a single-file extension. Compute `relTag` = relative path from root with `.ts`/`.js` stripped, OS separators normalized to `/` via `filepath.ToSlash`. Set Path=path, EntryFile=path, Kind="file". Extract JSDoc description. Return (ext, true). If name IS index.ts/index.js → return (nil, false) (it's a dir extension's entry, handled by the dir classifier).
  Define `classifyDir(root, path string) (*Extension, bool, bool)`: Given a DIRECTORY entry, return (ext, isExtension, shouldDescend). Check in order:
    (a) `package.json` with `pi.extensions` array naming ≥1 existing entry → package extension. EntryFile = first existing pi.extensions entry (resolved relative to dir). Kind="package". Return (ext, true, false) — DO NOT descend.
    (b) `index.ts` exists → dir extension. EntryFile=index.ts. Kind="dir". Return (ext, true, false) — DO NOT descend.
    (c) `index.js` exists → dir extension. EntryFile=index.js. Kind="dir". Return (ext, true, false) — DO NOT descend.
    (d) None of the above → plain directory. Return (nil, false, true) — DO descend (category folder).
  For recognized extensions (a-c), compute relTag = dir path relative to root, normalized to `/` (NO .ts/.js stripping for dirs). Parse package.json metadata if present. Extract JSDoc from entryFile if no package.json description.
  TEST: the PRD §7.1 worked example: gate.ts (file), git-checkpoint/index.ts+utils.ts (dir, NOT recursed), summarizer/package.json+src/index.ts (package, NOT recursed), writing/reddit-poster.ts (file in plain category dir), platform/linux/index.ts (nested dir). Verify exactly 5 entries with correct relTags: gate, git-checkpoint, summarizer, writing/reddit-poster, platform/linux.
4. OUTPUT: classifyFile/classifyDir functions consumed by Index() in P1.M2.T3.
5. DOCS: none — internal logic; discovery rules documented in README §5 (Mode B final task).

tasks.title: internal/discover — Index() walk + sort
tasks.description: Tie the classification logic into the public Index() entrypoint that walks the extensions dir, classifies entries, applies the descend rule, parses metadata, and returns a sorted []Extension.

subtasks.title: Index() function with WalkDir + SkipDir + stat-guard + sort
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_19b4b465824d/architecture/architecture_mapping.md §3d. Unlike skilldozer's simple WalkDir-for-SKILL.md, weave must use WalkDir with SkipDir to implement classify-then-descend. Stat-guard the root BEFORE WalkDir (skilldozer pattern: a missing root is otherwise swallowed by the per-entry error handler).
2. INPUT: classifyFile/classifyDir from P1.M2.T2.S1. Extension/BuildExtension/parsePackageJSON/ExtractJSDoc from P1.M2.T1.
3. LOGIC: Create `internal/discover/index.go`. Implement `func Index(extensionsDir string) ([]Extension, error)`:
  - `root, err := filepath.Abs(extensionsDir)` — make absolute first.
  - Stat-guard: `os.Stat(root)`; if missing or not a dir, return error.
  - Walk with `filepath.WalkDir(root, callback)`:
    - Skip root itself (WalkDir visits it first).
    - For each entry, check `err` first (skip unreadable entries, continue walking).
    - If FILE: call classifyFile. If it returns an extension, append to results.
    - If DIR: call classifyDir. If it returns an extension, append and return `filepath.SkipDir` (prevent descent into recognized extension dirs). If it returns shouldDescend=true, return nil (WalkDir descends naturally).
  - Sort results by RelTag (ascending) for deterministic output.
  - Empty store → nil slice, nil error.
  Malformed package.json does NOT abort the walk (unparseable JSON → build Extension with empty metadata; check will flag it). This mirrors skilldozer's lenient parse policy.
  TEST: the PRD §7.1 worked example. Empty dir. Dir with only plain subdirs (no entries). Nested category dirs.
4. OUTPUT: `discover.Index(dir) ([]Extension, error)` — the public catalog builder consumed by main.go (all modes), resolve, search, check.
5. DOCS: none — internal function; documented in README §5 (Mode B final task).

tasks.title: internal/ui — table formatting (TAG/NAME/DESCRIPTION)
tasks.description: Port skilldozer's internal/ui nearly verbatim. Renders the human-readable catalog table for --list and --search.

subtasks.title: PrintList + padRight + wrapWords + displayWidth
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_19b4b465824d/architecture/architecture_mapping.md §5. Port skilldozer's internal/ui/ui.go verbatim. Only change: the '(missing)' sentinel logic (skilldozer checked HasFM; weave checks Description == ""). PRD §7.3 item 3: empty description renders as '(none)' in --list.
2. INPUT: discover.Extension type from P1.M2.T1.S1.
3. LOGIC: Create `internal/ui/ui.go`. Port from skilldozer:
  - ANSI constants: ansiReset=`\x1b[0m`, ansiBold=`\x1b[1m`, ansiCyan=`\x1b[36m`.
  - `const descWrapWidth = 40`.
  - `func PrintList(w io.Writer, exts []discover.Extension, useColor bool)`: Compute column widths from plain content. Header row (bold labels): TAG, NAME, DESCRIPTION. Data rows: TAG=ext.RelTag (cyan when colored), NAME=ext.Name or '(none)', DESCRIPTION=ext.Description (TrimSpace'd) or '(none)' when empty. Word-wrap description to descWrapWidth; continuation lines leave TAG/NAME blank.
  - `func displayWidth(s string) int`: `utf8.RuneCountInString(s)`.
  - `func padRight(s string, n int) string`: Right-pad with spaces to display width.
  - `func wrapWords(s string, width int) []string`: Word-wrap at spaces; long words on own line; empty→single empty line.
  Change from skilldozer: description sentinel is '(none)' (PRD §7.3), not '(missing)'. The check is `strings.TrimSpace(ext.Description) == ""` → render '(none)'.
  Type signature: takes `[]discover.Extension` (not `[]discover.Skill`).
  TEST: table with various tag/name/description lengths; color on/off; empty description → '(none)'; long description wrapping; unicode tags.
4. OUTPUT: `ui.PrintList(w, exts, useColor)` consumed by main.go --list and --search modes.
5. DOCS: none — internal formatter; --list output format documented in README §4 (Mode B final task).

tasks.title: main.go wiring --list
tasks.description: Add the --list dispatch branch to run(), wiring extdir.Find() → discover.Index() → ui.PrintList().

subtasks.title: --list mode dispatch in run()
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Port skilldozer's `if c.list { ... }` branch from main.go run(). The data flow is Find() → Index() → PrintList().
2. INPUT: extdir.Find() from P1.M1.T3.S3. discover.Index() from P1.M2.T3.S1. ui.PrintList() from P1.M2.T4.S1. parseArgs already handles --list/-l (from P1.M1.T4.S1).
3. LOGIC: In `main.go run()`, add a branch (before the tag-resolution fallback, after --path):
  ```go
  if c.list {
      dir, _, err := extdir.Find()
      if err != nil { fmt.Fprintln(stderr, err); return 1 }
      exts, err := discover.Index(dir)
      if err != nil { fmt.Fprintln(stderr, err); return 1 }
      if len(exts) == 0 { fmt.Fprintln(stderr, "no extensions found in "+dir); return 1 }
      ui.PrintList(stdout, exts, isTerminal(stdout) && !c.noColor)
      return 0
  }
  ```
  Color is on when stdout is a TTY AND --no-color is not set (PRD §6.2). --file/--relative do NOT apply to --list (it prints a TABLE, not paths — PRD §6.2).
  TEST: run with example extension in store → table with TAG/NAME/DESCRIPTION. Empty store → exit 1, stderr message. Unconfigured → exit 1, stderr 'weave is not configured'.
4. OUTPUT: `weave --list` works end-to-end.
5. DOCS: none — --list usage documented in README §4 (Mode B final task).

milestones.title: Tag resolution & modifiers (PRD §7.2, build order step 3)
milestones.description: Implement tag → extension resolution with the 4-step precedence (canonical → basename → name → alias), and wire the canonical `weave <tag>` path printer with the --file/--relative/--all modifiers and the critical §6.4 atomicity contract.

tasks.title: internal/resolve — tag → extension resolution (PRD §7.2)
tasks.description: Port skilldozer's internal/resolve nearly verbatim. The 4-step precedence is identical; only the type changes (Skill → Extension).

subtasks.title: Resolve() with 4-step precedence + UnknownError + AmbiguousError
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_19b4b465824d/architecture/architecture_mapping.md §4. Port skilldozer's internal/resolve/resolve.go verbatim with type change. The 4-step precedence (PRD §7.2) is IDENTICAL to skilldozer's.
2. INPUT: discover.Extension type from P1.M2.T1.S1.
3. LOGIC: Create `internal/resolve/resolve.go`. Port from skilldozer:
  - `type MatchKind int` (Canonical, Basename, Name, Alias) + String().
  - `type Result struct { Extension discover.Extension; Match MatchKind }`.
  - `type UnknownError struct { Tag string }` → `Error()`: `unknown extension tag %q`.
  - `type AmbiguousError struct { Tag string; Candidates []string }` → `Error()`: `ambiguous extension tag %q matches: <candidates>`.
  - `func Resolve(tag string, exts []discover.Extension) (Result, error)`: 4-step precedence:
    1. Exact RelTag match → return (Result{ext, Canonical}, nil).
    2. Basename match (final '/'-component of RelTag) → if 1 hit return it; if >1 return AmbiguousError.
    3. Name match (ext.Name, only non-empty) → if 1 hit return it; if >1 return AmbiguousError.
    4. Alias match (in ext.Aliases) → if 1 hit return it; if >1 return AmbiguousError.
    5. Nothing → UnknownError.
  An ambiguity at any step SHORT-CIRCUITS (later steps NOT consulted).
  Port `collectMatches`, `basename`, `sortedRelTags` helpers verbatim (type change only: Skill → Extension).
  TEST: exact tag, basename fallback (writing/reddit-poster ← reddit-poster), name fallback (@my-org/summarizer ← summarizer dir), alias fallback, ambiguous basename → AmbiguousError with sorted candidates, unknown tag → UnknownError.
4. OUTPUT: `resolve.Resolve(tag, exts) (Result, error)` consumed by main.go tag-resolution loop.
5. DOCS: none — internal function; resolution rules documented in README §5 (Mode B final task).

tasks.title: main.go wiring <tag>, -f, --all, --relative
tasks.description: Wire the canonical `weave <tag> [<tag>...]` resolution loop with the §6.4 atomicity contract (buffer all paths, flush only if all resolve), the --file/--relative/--all modifiers, and the extensionPath() helper that distinguishes Path (default) from EntryFile (--file).

subtasks.title: Tag resolution loop + extensionPath() + atomicity + modifiers
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Port skilldozer's tag-resolution loop and skillPath() helper from main.go. Key difference: weave's extensionPath() distinguishes Path (resolvable, default) from EntryFile (the .ts/.js pi loads, --file output). In skilldozer, Dir was always the dir and SourceFile was always Dir+"/SKILL.md"; in weave, Path can be a FILE or DIR and EntryFile is derived per Kind.
2. INPUT: resolve.Resolve() from P1.M3.T1.S1. discover.Index() from P1.M2.T3.S1. extdir.Find() from P1.M1.T3.S3. parseArgs already handles tags, -f, --all, --relative (from P1.M1.T4.S1).
3. LOGIC: In `main.go`, implement:
  `func extensionPath(ext discover.Extension, extensionsDir string, c config) string`:
    - Default: `ext.Path` (the resolvable path — file for files, dir for dirs).
    - --file: `ext.EntryFile` (the .ts/.js file pi loads).
    - --relative: `filepath.Rel(extensionsDir, chosen)` — path relative to extensions dir.
    - --file --relative: combine (EntryFile relative to extensions dir).
  In `run()`, add the `--all` branch (before tags): Index(), iterate sorted exts, print `extensionPath(ext, dir, c)` per line. Exit 0 even for empty store.
  In `run()`, add the tag-resolution branch: Index(), then for each tag in c.tags: call resolve.Resolve; on error, print to stderr and set hadErr=true; on success, buffer the path. ATOMICITY (PRD §6.4): if ANY tag fails, print one error per problem to stderr, print NOTHING to stdout, return 1. If all resolve, flush buffered paths to stdout (one per line, input order), return 0.
  TEST: single tag → absolute path. Multiple tags → one per line, input order. Unknown tag → nothing on stdout, error on stderr, exit 1. Mixed good+bad tags → nothing on stdout, error on stderr, exit 1. --file on single-file ext → the file itself. --file on dir ext → index.ts. --file on package ext → first pi.extensions entry. --all → sorted by tag. --relative → relative paths. Absolute-path contract: output starts with '/'.
4. OUTPUT: `weave <tag>`, `weave -f <tag>`, `weave --all`, `weave --relative` all work end-to-end.
5. DOCS: none — CLI usage documented in README §4 (Mode B final task).

milestones.title: Search, validation & init (PRD §6.1 search/check, §9, §8.2, build order step 4)
milestones.description: Implement --search (substring filter over 6 metadata fields), check (validation report with ERROR/WARN rules), and the weave init first-run setup command (interactive store selection, create+seed+writeconfig).

tasks.title: internal/search — substring search
tasks.description: Port skilldozer's internal/search verbatim. Filters []Extension by case-insensitive substring over tag, name, description, keywords, aliases, category.

subtasks.title: Search() + matches() over 6 fields
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_19b4b465824d/architecture/architecture_mapping.md §6. Port skilldozer's internal/search/search.go verbatim. Type change: Skill → Extension. The 6 searchable fields are the same (PRD §6.1 --search).
2. INPUT: discover.Extension type from P1.M2.T1.S1.
3. LOGIC: Create `internal/search/search.go`. Port:
  - `func Search(query string, exts []discover.Extension) []discover.Extension`: Lowercase query once, filter exts where matches() returns true. Preserve input order (Index already sorts). Empty query matches all.
  - `func matches(q string, ext discover.Extension) bool`: Case-insensitive substring of RelTag, Name, Description, any Keyword (individually), any Alias (individually), or Category.
  Keywords/aliases tested INDIVIDUALLY (not joined) for boundary safety.
  TEST: search by tag substring, name substring, description substring, keyword, alias, category; empty query matches all; no match returns empty slice.
4. OUTPUT: `search.Search(query, exts) []Extension` consumed by main.go --search mode.
5. DOCS: none — internal function; --search documented in README §4 (Mode B final task).

tasks.title: internal/check — validation report (PRD §9)
tasks.description: Implement the validation rules from PRD §9. Different from skilldozer (which checked frontmatter/name rules); weave checks relTag duplicates, entryFile existence, package.json validity, deps-installed, and description presence.

subtasks.title: Check() with all §9 rules + Report/ExtensionReport/Finding types
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_19b4b465824d/architecture/architecture_mapping.md §7. Port the Report/SkillReport(→ExtensionReport)/Finding/Severity STRUCTURE from skilldozer. The validation RULES are different (PRD §9). check re-parses package.json per entry to distinguish 'unparseable' from 'missing' (mirrors skilldozer's re-parse of frontmatter).
2. INPUT: discover.Extension + discover.Index() from P1.M2. discover.parsePackageJSON (needs to be accessible — export it or re-parse in check).
3. LOGIC: Create `internal/check/check.go`. Port structure:
  - `type Severity int` (LevelOK, LevelWarn, LevelError) + String().
  - `type Finding struct { Level Severity; Message string }`.
  - `type ExtensionReport struct { Extension discover.Extension; Findings []Finding }`.
  - `type Report struct { BySkill []ExtensionReport; Errors int; Warnings int }` + `HasErrors() bool`.
  Implement `func Check(exts []discover.Extension) Report` with three passes:
  Pass 1 (per-extension local checks):
    - Re-parse package.json: if present but unparseable JSON → ERROR 'package.json is not valid JSON'.
    - If ext.EntryFile does not exist on disk → ERROR 'entry file does not exist: <path>'.
    - If ext.Kind == 'package' AND package.json has non-empty dependencies AND dir has no node_modules/ → WARN 'dependencies declared but node_modules/ not found'.
    - If ext.Description == '' (no package.json description AND no JSDoc) → WARN 'no description (neither package.json description nor leading JSDoc)'.
  Pass 2 (global checks):
    - Duplicate RelTag across entries → ERROR per duplicate, naming the other tag(s).
    - Top-level non-entry directory with zero discoverable entries at any depth → WARN 'empty category folder: <dir>'.
  Pass 3: tally errors/warnings.
  Empty store is clean (0/0/0 → exit 0).
  TEST: clean store (all OK); duplicate relTag; missing entryFile; unparseable package.json; deps without node_modules; no description; empty category dir.
4. OUTPUT: `check.Check(exts) Report` consumed by main.go check mode and runInit.
5. DOCS: none — internal function; check output format documented in README §4 (Mode B final task).

tasks.title: main.go wiring --search, check
tasks.description: Add --search and check dispatch branches to run().

subtasks.title: --search dispatch + check dispatch in run()
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Port skilldozer's --search and check dispatch branches from main.go run().
2. INPUT: search.Search() from P1.M4.T1.S1. check.Check() from P1.M4.T2.S1. ui.PrintList() from P1.M2.T4.S1. discover.Index() and extdir.Find(). parseArgs already handles --search/-s and check (from P1.M1.T4.S1).
3. LOGIC: In `main.go run()`, add:
  --search branch (after --list, before check): Find() → Index() → search.Search(query, exts) → if empty, stderr 'no extensions matched <query>', exit 1; else ui.PrintList(stdout, matched, isTerminal && !noColor), exit 0. --file/--relative do NOT apply (table output).
  check branch: Find() → Index() → check.Check(exts) → render: for each ExtensionReport, if no findings print 'OK   <relTag> (<name or basename>)'; for each finding print '<LEVEL> <relTag> (<name>): <message>'. Summary line: 'N extensions, M errors, K warnings'. Print to STDOUT (pipeable). Exit 0 if no errors, 1 if any ERROR. check is NOT subject to §6.4's 'nothing on stdout' (it's a report, not a path emitter).
  TEST: --search with matching/non-matching queries. check on clean store → exit 0, all OK. check with problems → exit 1, ERROR/WARN lines.
4. OUTPUT: `weave --search <q>` and `weave check` work end-to-end.
5. DOCS: none — CLI usage documented in README §4 (Mode B final task).

tasks.title: weave init command (PRD §8.2)
tasks.description: Implement the first-run setup: interactive/non-interactive store selection, dir creation, example seeding, config writing, and reporting. The bare `weave <tag>` path NEVER reaches here (prompt safety).

subtasks.title: chooseStore + resolveStore (store selection logic)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Port skilldozer's chooseStore/resolveStore/readPrompt/stdinIsTerminal from main.go. The pure decision logic (chooseStore) is fully testable with injected dependencies. PRD §8.2: if cwd already looks like a store (HasExtensionEntry), default = cwd; else default = $XDG_DATA_HOME/weave/extensions.
2. INPUT: extdir.HasExtensionEntry from P1.M1.T3.S1. config.DefaultStore() from P1.M1.T2.S1.
3. LOGIC: In `main.go`, implement:
  `func stdinIsTerminal() bool`: stat os.Stdin, check ModeCharDevice.
  `func readPrompt(r *bufio.Reader, w io.Writer, label, def string) (string, error)`: Print '[label] [def]: ', read one line, return trimmed or def on empty/EOF.
  `func chooseStore(haveStore, cwd string, isTTY bool, defaultStore string, prompt func(label, def string) (string, error)) (string, error)`: (1) If haveStore != '' (from `init <dir>` or `--store <dir>`) → return it verbatim, no prompt. (2) Auto-detect default: if extdir.HasExtensionEntry(cwd) → default = cwd; else default = defaultStore. (3) If !isTTY → return default, no prompt. (4) If isTTY → prompt 'Where should weave keep your extensions?'. Empty/EOF → default; typed path → override.
  `func resolveStore(haveStore string) (string, error)`: Supply real deps (os.Getwd, config.DefaultStore, stdinIsTerminal, bufio prompt), return chooseStore result absolutized via filepath.Abs.
  TEST: chooseStore with haveStore set → returns it, no prompt. chooseStore with isTTY=false → returns default, no prompt. chooseStore with isTTY=true and cwd containing extensions → default is cwd. chooseStore with prompt returning '' → returns default. chooseStore with prompt returning typed path → returns it.
4. OUTPUT: chooseStore + resolveStore functions consumed by runInit in S2.
5. DOCS: none — init behavior documented in README §3/§7 (Mode B final task).

subtasks.title: setupStore + runInit + exampleExtensionTemplate + init dispatch in run()
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Port skilldozer's setupStore/runInit from main.go. Key difference: seed `example.ts` (single-file) into the store root, NOT `example/SKILL.md` into a subdirectory. The template is the PRD §11 example extension body (a string constant). PRD §8.2 step 3: 'seed example.ts as a copy-paste template (a string constant compiled into the binary — not go:embed)'.
2. INPUT: resolveStore from P1.M4.T4.S1. config.Save/Path from P1.M1.T2.S1. extdir.Find from P1.M1.T3.S3. discover.Index from P1.M2.T3.S1. check.Check from P1.M4.T2.S1.
3. LOGIC: In `main.go`:
  Define `const exampleExtensionTemplate = <PRD §11 body>`. The §11 example.ts has no backticks in its content (the JSDoc + import + function), so a Go raw string literal works. Verify by checking: the content is the JSDoc comment + `import type { ExtensionAPI }...` + `export default function (pi: ExtensionAPI) {...}`. If any backticks are present, splice with `+ "` + "`" + `...`.
  `func setupStore(store, configPath string) (seeded bool, err error)`: (a) MkdirAll(store, 0o755). (b) ReadDir(store); if empty, WriteFile(filepath.Join(store, "example.ts"), []byte(exampleExtensionTemplate), 0o644); seeded=true. (c) Non-empty → adopt in place, never clobber. (d) config.Save(configPath, File{Store: store}).
  `func runInit(c config, stdout, stderr io.Writer) int`: (1) resolveStore(c.initStore). (2) config.Path(). (3) setupStore(store, cfgPath). (4) Report: 'Seeded example extension at <path>' or 'Adopted existing store at <path>' to stderr. (5) extdir.Find() → print dir to stdout (PRD §6.1: stdout = configured store path). Print '(found via <src>)' to stderr. (6) discover.Index + check.Check → print check report to stdout. Exit 0 (check findings don't change init exit code).
  In `run()`, the `if c.init` dispatch (before the normal mode ladder) calls runInit. init is exclusive (exclusivityError in M5 rejects init+other).
  TEST: init --store /tmp/test → creates /tmp/test, seeds example.ts, writes config, exit 0. init in a dir with existing extensions → adopts, no clobber. init interactively → prompts, Enter accepts default. Bare `weave x` NEVER reaches init.
4. OUTPUT: `weave init`, `weave init <dir>`, `weave init --store <dir>` all work.
5. DOCS: none — init usage documented in README §3 (Mode B final task).

milestones.title: CLI polish & error semantics (PRD §6.3, §6.4, build order step 5)
milestones.description: Finalize the CLI contract: --help usage text, precedence ordering (--help > --version > unknown flag > exclusivity > dispatch), unknown flag → exit 2, mutual exclusivity checking, and the no-args default (usage to stderr, exit 1).

tasks.title: Full CLI contract — --help, precedence, exclusivity, error semantics
tasks.description: Complete the §6.3/§6.4 CLI matrix: --help text, precedence ordering, mutual exclusivity rules, unknown flag exit 2, and no-args default behavior.

subtasks.title: --help usage text + run() precedence + exclusivityError + unknown-flag exit 2 + no-args default
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Port skilldozer's usageText, run() precedence ladder, and exclusivityError from main.go. PRD §6.3: '--help / --version take precedence over everything else'. PRD §6.3: 'Mixing <tag> with --list/--search/--all is an error (exit 2)'. PRD §6 header: 'Unknown flags ⇒ error + exit 2'. PRD §6.3: no args + no flag → usage to stderr, exit 1.
2. INPUT: run() from P1.M1.T4.S1 (grown through M2-M4). parseArgs already captures unknownFlag (from P1.M1.T4.S1).
3. LOGIC: In `main.go`:
  Define `const usageText = <full usage block>` (PRD §6.1/§6.3, mirroring skilldozer's structure but for weave: binary name `weave`, canonical `pi -e "$(weave <tag>)"` one-liner, all §6.1 commands/flags, exit code summary). Define `func usage() string { return usageText }`.
  In `run()`, implement the precedence ladder at the TOP (before mode dispatch):
    1. `if c.help { fmt.Fprint(stdout, usage()); return 0 }` — help wins (to STDOUT, exit 0).
    2. `if c.version { fmt.Fprintf(stdout, "weave %s\n", version); return 0 }`.
    3. `if c.unknownFlag != "" { fmt.Fprintf(stderr, "weave: unknown flag '%s'\n", c.unknownFlag); return 2 }`.
    4. `if bad, msg := exclusivityError(c); bad { fmt.Fprintln(stderr, msg); return 2 }`.
    5. `if c.init { return runInit(c, stdout, stderr) }`.
    6. Normal mode dispatch (--path → --list → --search → check → --all → tags).
    7. No recognized mode → `fmt.Fprint(stderr, usage()); return 1`.
  Implement `func exclusivityError(c config) (bad bool, msg string)`: Check: (a) 2+ of {path, list, searchMode, all} active → error. (b) tags + (list/searchMode/all) → error. (c) check + tags → error. (d) check + (path/list/searchMode/all) → error. (e) init + tags → error. (f) init + (check/list/searchMode/all/path) → error. --file/--relative/--no-color are modifiers, never trigger exclusivity.
  TEST: --help → usage to stdout, exit 0. --version → version to stdout, exit 0. --bogus → 'unknown flag' to stderr, exit 2. --list --search → exclusivity error, exit 2. tag --list → exclusivity error, exit 2. check tag → exclusivity error, exit 2. No args → usage to stderr, exit 1. --help --version → help wins (exit 0, usage on stdout). --bogus --help → help wins.
4. OUTPUT: Full §6.3/§6.4 CLI contract enforced. All acceptance criteria for error semantics pass.
5. DOCS: none — CLI contract documented in README §4 (Mode B final task). The usageText IS the user-facing help; it is self-documenting.

milestones.title: Example extension, packaging, docs & completions (build order steps 6-8)
milestones.description: Ship the one example extension, verify all §13 acceptance criteria pass, create install.sh, README.md, shell completions, and the final documentation sync.

tasks.title: Example extension + acceptance criteria (PRD §11, §13)
tasks.description: Ship the one example extension and verify every §13 acceptance gate passes end-to-end.

subtasks.title: extensions/example.ts + run §13 acceptance verification
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: The example extension template already exists as `exampleExtensionTemplate` in main.go (from P1.M4.T4.S2). This subtask creates the ON-DISK version at `extensions/example.ts` (the repo asset) and verifies all §13 acceptance criteria. Both copies MUST be identical to the PRD §11 body.
2. INPUT: The built `weave` binary (all features through M5).
3. LOGIC: Create `extensions/example.ts` with the EXACT content from PRD §11 (the JSDoc comment + import + export default function). The leading JSDoc is the description that --list displays. Run and verify ALL §13 acceptance criteria:
  - `go build -o weave . && echo OK`
  - `./weave --version` prints `weave <something>`
  - `test "$(./weave --path)" = "$PWD/extensions"` (sibling-of-binary)
  - `./weave --list` shows the `example` extension
  - `test -f "$(./weave example)"` (resolves to real file)
  - `test -f "$(./weave -f example)"` (-f on single-file = the file itself)
  - Unknown tag: nothing on stdout, exit 1
  - Absolute-path contract: output starts with '/'
  - `./weave check` exits 0, reports example as OK
  - Dir + package extension styles resolve (temp scaffolding from §13)
  - Symlink install resolves back to repo
  - Config + first-run: unconfigured hint, non-interactive init
  - `pi --no-extensions -e "$(./weave example)" -p "briefly confirm..."` loads ONLY via -e
  If any criterion fails, fix the implementation (NOT the PRD). The pi end-to-end test requires pi on PATH; if unavailable, document it as deferred.
4. OUTPUT: `extensions/example.ts` on disk + verified acceptance. The full §13 suite passes.
5. DOCS: none — example extension is self-documenting via its JSDoc; documented in README §5/§6 (Mode B final task).

tasks.title: install.sh (PRD §12.1)
tasks.description: Create the install script that builds the binary with version ldflags and symlinks it into PATH.

subtasks.title: install.sh (build + symlink + PATH check + verify)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Port skilldozer's install.sh with renames. PRD §12.1 defines 7 steps. The symlink is load-bearing for §8.3 sibling-of-binary resolution. Env var: `weave_INSTALL_BIN`.
2. INPUT: The built `weave` binary from P1.M6.T1.S1.
3. LOGIC: Create `install.sh` (bash, `#!/usr/bin/env bash`, `set -euo pipefail`). Implement PRD §12.1 steps:
  1. `cd` to script's own dir (repo root) via `$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)`.
  2. Verify `go` on PATH; if not, print install instructions to stderr, exit 1.
  3. `go build -trimpath -ldflags "-s -w -X main.version=$(git describe --tags --always 2>/dev/null || echo dev)" -o weave .`
  4. Pick target bin dir: `$weave_INSTALL_BIN` → `$HOME/.local/bin` (if present/creatable) → `/usr/local/bin` (if writable, else needs sudo).
  5. `ln -sfn "$SCRIPT_DIR/weave" "$TARGET/weave"` (SYMLINK, not copy; absolute target; -n for existing symlink-to-dir).
  6. Ensure target on PATH; if not, print exact `export PATH=...` for detected shell (bash/zsh/fish).
  7. Print verify command: `weave example`.
  TEST: run install.sh in a temp clone → binary builds, symlink created, `weave --version` works via symlink, sibling-of-binary rule finds extensions/.
4. OUTPUT: `install.sh` at repo root.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] install.sh is self-documenting (echo statements guide the user). Install instructions also appear in README §3 (Mode B final task P1.M6.T5).

tasks.title: Shell completions (PRD §14)
tasks.description: Ship completions for bash, zsh, fish. They complete subcommands/flags and tags (dynamically via `weave --all`).

subtasks.title: weave.bash + _weave (zsh) + weave.fish
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Port skilldozer's completions/ directory verbatim with renames. Binary name: `weave` (not `skilldozer`). Tag completion calls `weave --relative --all` (dynamic, disk-derived). Flag set is frozen to main.go parseArgs(). All three files must stay in LOCKSTEP with the flag matrix.
2. INPUT: The §6.1/§6.2 flag matrix (frozen by P1.M5.T1.S1).
3. LOGIC: Create three files:
  `completions/weave.bash`: Port skilldozer.bash. Function `_weave_completion`. Flag completion when token starts with '-': compgen -W with all long+short flags (--version -v --help -h --path -p --list -l --all -a --file -f --relative --no-color --search -s). --search/-s after → no completion (free-text). Walk earlier words: `check` is exclusive → suppress tags. Tags from `weave --relative --all`. Offer `check` only as first positional.
  `completions/_weave`: Port _skilldozer. `#compdef weave`. Tags from `weave --relative --all`. Flag list with descriptions. `:query:` for --search/-s (value-taking). check offered only as first token; suppressed after check seen.
  `completions/weave.fish`: Port skilldozer.fish. `complete -c weave -f`. Flag matrix with -s/-l/-d. check as first arg only. Dynamic tags via command substitution, suppressed after check or --search/-s.
  All three suppress file completion (weave takes tags/flags, not paths). All three call `weave --relative --all` at completion time for tags.
4. OUTPUT: Three completion files in `completions/`.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Each file has install instructions in its header comment. Installation also noted in README §3 (Mode B final task).

tasks.title: README.md (PRD §15)
tasks.description: Write the user-facing README following the §15 outline, mirroring skilldozer's README tone and structure.

subtasks.title: README.md (full §15 outline)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Mirror skilldozer's README tone and structure (see ~/projects/skilldozer/README.md). PRD §15 defines the 8-section outline. Use the write-tech-docs skill conventions: no em dashes, no marketing tell-words, no hedging.
2. INPUT: All implemented features (M1-M5). install.sh (P1.M6.T2.S1). Completions (P1.M6.T3.S1). The example extension (P1.M6.T1.S1).
3. LOGIC: Create `README.md` following PRD §15 outline:
  1. Title + one-liner: 'Standalone extension loader for pi — resolves an extension tag to an absolute path for `pi -e`.'
  2. Why: centralized extensions, NOT in any pi discovery location, loaded only on demand.
  3. Install: install.sh (symlink) / go install (first-class, with `weave init` note) / from-source. First run: `weave init`.
  4. Usage: canonical `pi -e "$(weave tag)"` example, multi-extension, `-f`, `--list`, `--search`, `--all`, `check`, `--path`, `init`.
  5. How extensions are organized: single file (gate.ts), dir (foo/index.ts), package (foo/package.json + pi.extensions); tag = relative path (.ts/.js stripped for files); discovery rules (§7 summary).
  6. Adding an extension: drop a .ts file or dir under extensions/; optional package.json; `weave check`.
  7. How weave finds the store: §8 summary — `weave init` writes config; `weave_EXTENSIONS_DIR` overrides; sibling/walk-up are dev fallbacks. Document `weave_CONFIG` and `weave_EXTENSIONS_DIR` env vars.
  8. Constraints: no catalog index; settings config file is fine; never auto-discovered; loads only via `-e`.
  Also include a Shell Completions section (how to install bash/zsh/fish completions).
4. OUTPUT: `README.md` at repo root.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] This IS the documentation deliverable. It self-references all CLI flags, env vars, and behaviors implemented across M1-M6.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation (§5 Mode B)

subtasks.title: Final doc sweep — verify README/features/env-vars/overview match implementation
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: This is the §5 Mode B catch-all. Per-feature docs (Mode A) rode with each implementing subtask (install instructions in install.sh/completions, example extension JSDoc, usage in README). This task verifies cross-cutting documentation coherence now that the whole changeset is in place.
2. INPUT: All implementing subtasks (every subtask in P1.M1-P1.M6). The complete README from P1.M6.T4.S1.
3. LOGIC: Audit and fix documentation drift across the whole changeset:
  - README.md: verify ALL §6.1 commands/flags are documented (weave <tag>, --all, --list, --search, check, init, --path, --help, --version) and ALL §6.2 modifiers (--file, --no-color, --relative).
  - README.md: verify env vars documented: `weave_EXTENSIONS_DIR` (§8.3 rule 1), `weave_CONFIG` (§8.1 config override), `weave_INSTALL_BIN` (§12.1 install override).
  - README.md: verify the constraints section (§15 item 8) accurately states: no catalog index (disk-discovered), settings file is fine, never auto-discovered by pi, loads only via -e.
  - README.md: verify install instructions cover all three paths (install.sh, go install, from-source) and mention `weave init` for go install users.
  - README.md: verify the canonical one-liner is `pi -e "$(weave <tag>)"` and the --no-extensions pattern is mentioned.
  - Verify .gitignore matches PRD §16 exactly (/weave, /dist, *.test, *.out, .DS_Store).
  - Verify no stale references to skilldozer internals or yaml.v3 in any user-facing doc.
  If any discrepancy is found, fix the doc (NOT the implementation, unless the implementation is wrong).
4. OUTPUT: Documentation is fully synchronized with the implementation. No drift.
5. DOCS: This IS the documentation sync task. It ensures the changeset ships with coherent, accurate docs.
